name: Vox Shifter
explanation: A formant-aware vocal pitch shifter in the PSOLA / phase-vocoder family. A twin-grain crossfaded delay-line pitch shifter moves the pitch (Shift, plus/minus 12 semitones) click-free, while an independent Formant control sweeps a pair of resonant vowel formants over the shifted voice, so you can pitch a voice up without the chipmunk formant collapse, or slide the formants for gender and vowel morphs. Mix blends dry and wet; Output trims level. Controls: Shift, Formant, Mix, Output.
